expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: scenario drafting distinguishes dashboard-level Google Sheets export paths, option combinations, and visible completion outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly covers all three: (1) dashboard-level paths and distinction: "Dashboard — Google Sheets export paths" and scenario "Distinguish dashboard-level Google Sheets export from report-level export entry points" with checks that "Dashboard-level and report-level export paths are distinguishable"; (2) option combinations: scenarios "Export option combinations — baseline set applied consistently" and "Export option combinations — invalid/unsupported combinations are blocked clearly"; (3) visible completion outcomes: multiple steps verify "Export job starts (progress / in-app status becomes visible)" and "A completion outcome is visible" plus success/failure, reruns, and sequential runs under "Dashboard — Completion outcomes and post-export visibility".

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The produced artifact is titled "Phase 4a — Scenario Draft (Subcategory-only)" and follows the Phase 4a style described in execution_notes.md: "Draft is subcategory-first" and uses nested atomic steps with observable verification leaves (e.g., action chain leading to checks like "Confirm action is disabled or a clear inline validation message is shown"). It also avoids canonical top-layer categories as noted: "Phase 4a intentionally avoids canonical top-layer categories".
